intent.goal: Give a single founder the operational leverage of a full company by routing any task to a specialized agent with persistent company context -- delivered as a Claude Code plugin
explicit_constraints: Delivered as a Claude Code plugin (claude plugin install soleur)

invariants.value: Claude Code plugin (installed via claude plugin install)
Single intent-routing command that classifies and dispatches to department agents
invariants.source: /soleur:go <what you want to do> -- classifies your intent and routes to the right workflow

invariants.value: Claude Code CLI session with persistent knowledge accumulation across sessions
invariants.source: compounding your company knowledge with every session -- every problem you solve makes the next one easier

invariants.value: 61 agents across 8 departments: Engineering, Finance, Marketing, Legal, Operations, Product, Sales, Support
invariants.source: 61 agents across engineering, finance, marketing, legal, operations, product, sales, and support

invariants.value: Compounding company knowledge store that accumulates across sessions
invariants.source: compounding your company knowledge with every session -- every problem you solve makes the next one easier

invariants.value: Single founder (solo) seeking leverage of a full organization
invariants.source: Soleur gives a single founder the leverage of a full organization

features: Company-as-a-Service framing: replaces hiring with agents
Single routing command abstracts 61 agents from the user
Persistent, compounding knowledge base as a core differentiator (not just stateless AI calls)
Full departmental coverage in one plugin (8 departments)
BSL 1.1 license signals commercial product, not open-source community tool
identity.why_distinctive: Most AI agent tools are either general-purpose (no domain specialization) or single-function (one department). Soleur's claim is full organizational coverage with a single entry point and a knowledge model that improves over time -- targeting the specific gap of the solo founder who cannot afford to hire.

term_flags.source_quote: compounding your company knowledge with every session -- every problem you solve makes the next one easier
term_flags.chosen_interpretation: A persistent, structured knowledge store (e.g., files or a database) that agents read and write to across sessions, accumulating decisions, context, and outputs
alternatives: The LLM context window grows larger with each session (stateful conversation memory only)
A user-maintained wiki or document that agents reference but do not autonomously update
term_flags.impact: The chosen interpretation implies a durable, agent-writable knowledge architecture. If it is only conversation memory, the 'compounding' claim is weaker and the session_medium and knowledge_model invariants change significantly.

founder_profile.inference_basis: The idea specifies a multi-agent architecture with 61 agents, an explicit routing workflow, a named TypeScript implementation, a BSL license, and a live GitHub repo -- indicating a technical founder with direct domain knowledge of AI agent systems.

strategic_signals.inference_notes: BSL 1.1 is a source-available commercial license that allows use but restricts competing hosting -- strongly implying a paid SaaS model. Distribution is explicitly a Claude Code plugin. Success metric inferred as usage (compound knowledge grows with sessions) rather than revenue, since no pricing or revenue signal appears in the idea description. Business model classified saas as the most likely monetization path under BSL, but this needs founder confirmation.
